concept.conceptName: Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration
Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration

en.name: Urinary phosphate
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urinary phosphate is the concentration of inorganic phosphate in a urine specimen — typically a spot (random) urine sample [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. ## Included Concepts The set lists four LOINC "Lab Test" concepts measuring the phosphate concentration in a spot urine specimen: - *3022229 / 13539-2 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot (random) urine sample. Most widely used LOINC code. - *3026729 / 2778-9 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *21493398 / 80611-7 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: used when the source does not specify whether the sample is spot or timed. - *3036691 / 35673-3 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for urinary phosphate are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Timed urine collections — different specimen (timed vs. spot) - *3021916 / 25973-9 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *3025776 / 21458-5 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *40760465 / 57348-5 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in 12 hour Urine* - *40760466 / 57349-3 — Phosphate [Mass/volume] in 2 hour Urine* ### Excretion rates — different measurand (amount per unit time, not a concentration) - *3010600 / 14881-7 — Phosphate [Moles/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3012636 / 2779-7 — Phosphate [Mass/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3041509 / 51604-7 — Phosphate [Moles/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *40758052 / 54922-0 — Phosphate [Mass/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *40762086 / 58993-7 — Phosphate [Mass/time] in 18 hour Urine* - *3032333 / 35263-3 — Phosphate [Mass or Moles] in 24 hour Urine* ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3034533 / 11141-9 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Urine* - *3033868 / 13795-0 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *3044348 / 34354-1 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine* - *3009615 / 25127-2 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *40760083 / 56964-0 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Ratio] in Urine* - *40760084 / 56965-7 — Phosphate/Creatinine [Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* ### Qualitative — different measurand (presence, not a concentration) - *3025766 / 2780-5 — Phosphate [Presence] in Urine* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urinary phosphate", "urine P", "urine PO4", "spot urine phosphate" without further specification: `3022229 / 13539-2 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Urine`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports a 24-hour (or other timed) urine collection**: do **not** map to this concept set. Timed urinary phosphate is out of scope. - **Source reports a phosphate-to-creatinine ratio**: do **not** map. These are different measurands. - **Do not confuse with**: phosphate in blood (separate concept set, "Phosphate in blood"), urinary phosphate excretion rate (mmol/24h), fractional excretion of phosphate (a derived value). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 13539-2 — Phosphate [Moles/volume] in Urine. Available: https://loinc.org/13539-2/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
